name: Crab Fritters
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, mix olive oil, minced garlic, curry powder, mayonnaise, sour cream, orange juice, sugar, lemon juice, and chutney. Let the batter stand at room temperature for 1 hour.
2. In a large bowl, combine crabmeat, breadcrumbs, two egg whites, and parsley. Season with salt and pepper. Divide mixture into 24 mounds and press each mound firmly into a ball.
3. Roll each crabmeat ball in flour; shake off excess. Beat remaining egg whites until stiff peaks form.
4. Heat oil in a deep fryer or heavy saucepan to 360°F (180°C).
5. Dip crab balls one at a time into the batter, coating completely. Carefully lower into the oil. Repeat with remaining crab balls, frying until pale and golden brown, about 5 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer fritters to paper towels to drain.
6. Serve with Curry Sauce. Makes 24 fritters.
Notes:
Do not overcrowd the fryer or saucepan when making these crab fritters. Make no more than 5 fritters at a time. Be certain to slide the fritters gently into the hot oil. Dropping them can cause a dangerous splash. Turn the fritters frequently as they fry, ensuring all sides are browned.
